title: A Calendar Of Sonnets - April
content:
No days such honored days as these! When yet
    Fair Aphrodite reigned, men seeking wide
    For some fair thing which should forever bide
    On earth, her beauteous memory to set
    In fitting frame that no age could forget,
    Her name in lovely April's name did hide,
    And leave it there, eternally allied
    To all the fairest flowers Spring did beget.
    And when fair Aphrodite passed from earth,
    Her shrines forgotten and her feasts of mirth,
    A holier symbol still in seal and sign,
    Sweet April took, of kingdom most divine,
    When Christ ascended, in the time of birth
    Of spring anemones, in Palestine.
meaning:
"A Calendar Of Sonnets - April" is a quintessential example of Helen Hunt Jackson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
